channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Utes sweep College of Charleston in NCAA Tournament
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Utah's incredible turnaround season isn't done yet. After going 11-19 in 2023, the Utes Volleyball team has advanced to the second round of the NCAA Tournament after a three-set sweep over the College of Charleston Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Move over Magic: Nuggets star Nikola Jokic goes past Johnson on career list with 139th triple-double
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — There are moments when Nikola Jokic does things on the court that seem to defy reason — almost magical. On Thursday, he passed a player who had the same remarkable skill.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NBA returning to China for pair of Suns-Nets preseason games in 2025, AP source says
item.description: The NBA is returning to China next season, striking a deal to play preseason games there more than five years after the league was effectively banned over Commissioner Adam Silver not punishing Daryl Morey for tweeting support of anti-government protesters in Hong Kong.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ashton Jeanty confirms departure from Boise State after this season
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Boise State running back and Heisman Trophy candidate Ashton Jeanty confirmed Thursday that this will be his last season at the university.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Blackhawks fire coach Luke Richardson in his 3rd season after league-worst start
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The Chicago Blackhawks fired coach Luke Richardson on Thursday, signaling their frustration with the state of the franchise's rebuilding project. Chicago has dropped four in a row to fall to an NHL-worst 8-16-2 on the season.
item.dc:creator: JAY COHEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: BYU loses in first round of NCAA Tournament
item.description: WEST LAFAYETTE, Ind. — The BYU women's volleyball team was making its 13th consecutive appearance in the NCAA Tournament. But for the first time since 2005, the Cougars are going home after losing in the first round.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: The A's clear the last major hurdle for a $1.75 billion Las Vegas stadium
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — The Las Vegas Stadium Authority approved lease, non-relocation and development documents Thursday to clear the last major hurdles for the Athletics to construct a $1.75 billion stadium on the Strip and bring Major League Baseball to the expanding sports market.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Manny Pacquiao, winner of titles in a record 8 weight classes, elected to Boxing Hall of Fame
item.description: Manny Pacquiao, who won titles in a record eight weight classes from flyweight to super welterweight while becoming one of boxing's biggest draws, was elected Thursday to the International Boxing Hall of Fame.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: North Carolina interviews Bill Belichick for head coaching job, AP sources say
item.description: North Carolina has interviewed former New England Patriots coach and six-time Super Bowl champion Bill Belichick for its head coaching position, two people with knowledge of the situation said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: AARON BEARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tottenham joins list of top Premier League teams to lose at Bournemouth as fans jeer Postecoglou
item.description: Manchester City, Arsenal, and now Tottenham. The list of top Premier League teams beaten at Bournemouth this season is growing. Dean Huijsen took advantage of Tottenham’s weakness at set pieces to head home a 17th-minute winner in Bournemouth’s 1-0 victory on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: STEVE DOUGLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Scottie Scheffler has new putting grip and trails Cameron Young by 3 in Bahamas
item.description: NASSAU, Bahamas (AP) — Scottie Scheffler brought a new putting grip to the Hero World Challenge and felt enough improvement to be satisfied with the result, a 5-under 67 that left him three shots behind Cameron Young on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill Belichick interviews for college football job after legendary coach leaves: report
item.description: After being off the NFL sidelines for the first time since 1975 this year, Bill Belichick has reportedly interviewed for the head coaching job at the University of North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Eric Bieniemy, once highly sought-after NFL head coaching prospect, out at UCLA as offensive coordinator
item.description: UCLA offensive coordinator Eric Bieniemy parted ways with the school after one year on the job as he hopes to return to the NFL as a coach again.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amber Glenn takes the lead in Grand Prix Final figure skating after Kaori Sakamoto falls
item.description: GRENOBLE, France (AP) — Competing with five Japanese skaters in the six-woman field, Amber Glenn of the United States continued her breakout season in figure skating by taking the lead in the short program at the Grand Prix Final on Thursday as world champion Kaori Sakamoto fell.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White Sox great 'Beltin'' Bill Melton dead at 79
item.description: The Chicago White Sox announced that Bill Melton, a former All-Star third baseman who played eight of his 10 MLB seasons with them, died from a brief illness at 79 years old.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Olympics leader Bach talks up working with President-elect Trump toward 2028 Los Angeles Games
item.description: GENEVA (AP) — Olympic leaders are “very confident and relaxed” about working with the incoming Trump administration ahead of the 2028 Los Angeles Summer Games, outgoing IOC president Thomas Bach said Thursday. U.S.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Luis Severino and Athletics agree to $67 million, 3-year contract, AP sources say
item.description: Two people familiar with the negotiations tell The Associated Press that right-hander Luis Severino and the Athletics have agreed to a $67 million, three-year contract.
item.dc:creator: Janie Mccauley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Aaron Rodgers says it 'ridiculous' to believe he has more to prove, still hopes to return to Jets next year
item.description: While Aaron Rodgers remains interested in a future with the Jets, it is unclear if the franchise has mutual feelings about the relationship continuing in 2025.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Golden State Valkyries unveil uniforms ahead of inaugural WNBA season
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (KRON) -- The Golden State Valkyries won’t take the court for their inaugural 2025 season until May 16, but fans are getting an early look at the WNBA team’s uniforms which were unveiled Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mense, KRON4

item.title: St. Pete City Council votes in favor of bond purchase to pay for Rays stadium
item.description: The St. Petersburg City Council voted Thursday in favor of purchasing bonds that would fund a portion of the new Rays stadium and the redevelopment of the Gas Plant District.
item.dc:creator: Alessandra Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: George Russell accuses Max Verstappen of bullying and threatening behavior as F1 feud deepens
item.description: ABU DHABI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Mercedes driver George Russell has accused Formula 1 champion Max Verstappen of bullying and threatening behavior as a dispute between the two at last week's race in Qatar deepened Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Aggies win MWC opener over Wyoming, 70-67
item.description: LOGAN, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - The undefeated streak is still going for the Aggies. Ian Martinez scored 17 of his 23 points in the second half to help Utah State defeat Wyoming 70-67 on Wednesday night at The Spectrum. Mason Falslev's layup with 35 seconds left stretched Utah State's lead to 70-64.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: SEC and Big Ten powers lead the way on signing day as prospects finalize their college selections
item.description: The arrival of the early signing period Wednesday offered a stark reminder for college football fans. If recruiting rankings offer any accurate gauge in the era of the transfer portal, those teams competing for College Football Playoff berths now should keep contending for years to come.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
